definitions: To arise or occur, especially unexpectedly.
To approach or move towards someone or something.
To grow out of the ground; to sprout.
To be presented for consideration or decision.
To reach a higher level or position.
To travel from a more southerly place to a more northerly place.
examples: That issue came up in the meeting.
He came up to me and asked a question.
The seeds are starting to come up.
The case will come up next month.
She came up through the ranks.
They came up from Florida for the summer.
